name: INFECTIOUS AORTITIS
definition: inflammation of aorta caused by bacterium / virus / fungus LUETIC AORTITIS

demographics.Prevalence: in 10-15% of untreated patients (accounts for death in ⅓)
demographics.Path: periaortitis (via lymphatics), mesaortitis (via vasa vasorum) = primarily disease of

dominant_finding: saccular (75%) / fusiform (25%) dilatation of ascending aorta:
discriminating_features: thickening of aortic wall + intramural gas (KEY FEATURE)
thick aortic wall ← fibrous + inflammatory tissue
saccular (75%) / fusiform (25%) dilatation of ascending aorta:
small saccular aneurysm often protrudes from fusiform aneurysm
fine pencil-like calcifications of intima (15-20%) in ascending aorta, late in disease

general: thickening of aortic wall + intramural gas (KEY FEATURE)
thick aortic wall ← fibrous + inflammatory tissue
saccular (75%) / fusiform (25%) dilatation of ascending aorta:
small saccular aneurysm often protrudes from fusiform aneurysm
fine pencil-like calcifications of intima (15-20%) in ascending aorta, late in disease

aorta may appear normal on imaging
clinical.Rx: empiric antibiotic therapy against S. aureus and gram-negative rods
